Fluoropolymer The modern non-stick pans were made using a coating of Teflon (polytetrafluoroethylene or PTFE ). PTFE was invented serendipitously by Roy Plunkett in 1938,[3][4] while working for a joint venture of the DuPont company. The substance was found to have several unique properties, including very good corrosion-resistance

http://localhost:8090/content/wikipedia_.description: ... PTFE -based composition is Teflon by Chemours,[4] a spin-off from DuPont, which originally invented the compound in 1938.[4] Polytetrafluoroethylene is a fluorocarbon solid, as it is a high-molecular-weight polymer consisting wholly of carbon and fluorine. PTFE is hydrophobic: neither water nor water-containing substances wet PTFE , as fluorocarbons exhibit only small London dispersion forces
